title.en: Simulate a Capital One transaction state machine
title.es: Simular una máquina de estados de transacciones de Capital One

statement.en:
At Capital One, payment transactions move through a defined lifecycle. Given a `String[] events` where each element is one of `"CHARGE"`, `"REFUND"`, `"DISPUTE"`, or `"RESOLVE"`, simulate the transaction state machine starting from `"PENDING"` and return the **final state** as a `String`.

Signature: `public String finalState(String[] events)`

Unknown event strings are treated as a no-op (the "any other" rule in the table). No exceptions are thrown for unknown inputs.

**Transition table:**

| Current state | Event | Next state |
|---|---|---|
| `PENDING` | `CHARGE` | `CHARGED` |
| `PENDING` | any other | `PENDING` (no-op) |
| `CHARGED` | `REFUND` | `REFUNDED` |
| `CHARGED` | `DISPUTE` | `DISPUTED` |
| `CHARGED` | any other | `CHARGED` (no-op) |
| `REFUNDED` | any event | `REFUNDED` (terminal) |
| `DISPUTED` | `RESOLVE` | `CHARGED` |
| `DISPUTED` | any other | `DISPUTED` (no-op) |

Examples:
- `["CHARGE", "DISPUTE", "RESOLVE", "REFUND"]` → `"REFUNDED"`
- `["CHARGE", "REFUND", "DISPUTE"]` → `"REFUNDED"` (`DISPUTE` is a no-op on terminal state)
- `["DISPUTE"]` → `"PENDING"` (no-op from `PENDING`)
- `[]` → `"PENDING"` (no events, initial state returned)
statement.es:
En Capital One, los pagos pasan por un ciclo de vida definido. Dado un arreglo `String[] events` donde cada elemento es uno de `"CHARGE"`, `"REFUND"`, `"DISPUTE"` o `"RESOLVE"`, simula la máquina de estados de transacciones comenzando desde `"PENDING"` y devuelve el **estado final** como `String`.

Firma: `public String finalState(String[] events)`

Las cadenas de eventos desconocidas se tratan como no-op (la regla "cualquier otro" en la tabla). No se lanzan excepciones para entradas desconocidas.

**Tabla de transiciones:**

| Estado actual | Evento | Estado siguiente |
|---|---|---|
| `PENDING` | `CHARGE` | `CHARGED` |
| `PENDING` | cualquier otro | `PENDING` (no-op) |
| `CHARGED` | `REFUND` | `REFUNDED` |
| `CHARGED` | `DISPUTE` | `DISPUTED` |
| `CHARGED` | cualquier otro | `CHARGED` (no-op) |
| `REFUNDED` | cualquier evento | `REFUNDED` (terminal) |
| `DISPUTED` | `RESOLVE` | `CHARGED` |
| `DISPUTED` | cualquier otro | `DISPUTED` (no-op) |

Ejemplos:
- `["CHARGE", "DISPUTE", "RESOLVE", "REFUND"]` → `"REFUNDED"`
- `["CHARGE", "REFUND", "DISPUTE"]` → `"REFUNDED"` (`DISPUTE` es no-op en estado terminal)
- `["DISPUTE"]` → `"PENDING"` (no-op desde `PENDING`)
- `[]` → `"PENDING"` (sin eventos, se devuelve el estado inicial)
